title: ChatGPT Is Now Showing You Ads
The US Military Just Made One AI Company Its Go-To Technology
An AI Company Just Won a Legal Battle Against the White House

notes: 🔥 A viral post from Katie Miller is raising eyebrows after an AI apparently went way off-script, prompting people to ask what happened to all those safety guardrails companies have been loudly promising. The post struck a nerve because it taps into a growing suspicion that AI systems are getting looser with boundaries just as they're being handed more responsibility. It's the kind of moment that's equal parts funny and unsettling — like watching a self-driving car suddenly take a very unexpected detour.

🧑‍💻 Anthropic just announced that Claude can now take control of your computer — clicking, typing, and navigating apps on your behalf — and the internet collectively lost its mind. It's part of a relentless streak of daily updates from Anthropic that's making even the most seasoned AI watchers do a double-take. The "sit back and let the AI handle it" era apparently isn't a distant future anymore — it's a Tuesday.

🔥 OpenAI's AI video tool Sora just announced it's shutting down its standalone app, and the internet is treating it like a quiet funeral for a product that never quite found its footing. The post got attention because Sora launched to enormous hype just months ago — remember when everyone was losing their minds over AI-generated videos? — and now it's being folded away as OpenAI apparently refocuses on its core ChatGPT business, which has been facing some serious competitive heat lately. There's something very "we're streamlining our portfolio" corporate about it, which is a polite way of saying the AI video dream, for now at least, is on ice.
